sutta: now a certain monk approached the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side and so seated he spoke thus to the exalted 1 lord what is the cause the reason why to the learned arend listener doubt arises not as to unexplained points and the buddha said verily it is by view stopping monk that doubt arises not to the learned iron listener as to the unexplained points does the tatara gather exists after that this is but a view issue thus the tatara gather exist not after death both exist and exist not after death neither exists nor exists not after death these are but view issues the unlearned average man understands not view understands not view origin understands not view stopping understands not the stopping the stepping of the way to view stopping for him view grows and he's not freed from birth old age from from from being born ageing dying from sorrow grief lamentation despair etc it's not free from ill i say but the learned aryan listener understands view its origin it's stopping the stepping of the way there too for him view is stopped and he's freed from being born ageing dying from sorrow lamentation pain grief and despair is free from illness thus knowing that seeing the learned aryan listener indeed explains not does that tagata exist after that and similar questions thus knowing that seeing the learned aren't listening with us is thus subject to the inexplicable as to the unexplained points thus knowing thus seeing the learned aryan disciple is not afraid trembles not wavers not shakes not nor false to quaking concerning these points does the tata gather exist after death etc these monks about which issues of craving are issues of perception are but illusions fancies about issues of grasping about a source of remorse the unlearned average man understands not these things their origin they're stopping and they're stepping of the way there too they grow and he's not freed from birth aging dying etc he's not free from dukkha i say but the learned aryan listener understands and is freed from dukkha i say thus knowing thus seeing he explains not these questions is subject to the inexplicable as to the unexplained points he's not afraid trembles not wavers not shakes not not false to quaking concerning these points verily among this is of course the reason why to the learned aryan listener doubt arises not as to the unexplained points this is about the views what the buddha calls a proliferation of use the ticket of use as long as we and the course and the course of having all these views is mentioned in 1 of the sutas it is due to having sakaya deity psychiatry is the view of being in the body as long as we have a view of a being in the body then we have all kinds of questions arising where did where did i come from where will i go after that how did this world arise who created me who created this world and things like mentioned here that's it does the buddha exist after death or the buddha not exist after death etc etc and all these views will stop them when we have uh abandoned this view of a being in the body and all doubt is abandoned okay the next suta is
